title: ALP SoM preset (metadata/e1m_modules/<SKU>.yaml) v1
description: Schema for the per-SKU SoM preset.  The SDK's loader resolves the customer's board.yaml `som.sku` to the matching preset here.  Every released-MPN preset shares ONE canonical shape: the top-level key set, `memory:`, `on_module:`, `inference:`, and `status:` are closed (additionalProperties: false) so cross-family key drift fails validation instead of passing silently.  The only deliberately-open spots are the ones where genuine per-family variance is legitimate, and each carries its own description saying why: `capabilities:` (open set of accelerator flags matched against library hw-backends.yaml), `i2c_devices` bus KEYS (bus identifiers differ per family), `bus_pads` (per-silicon pad naming), and `helper_firmware[].flash_args` (shape follows the flash backend).

silicon_variant.description: SoC variant order code matching the vendor's datasheet (e.g. `AE302F80F55D5LE` for Alif E3, `R9A09G056N44GBG` for Renesas RZ/V2N, `MIMX9352xxxxM` for NXP i.MX 93 — the latter is partial pending the HW config writeup, encoded as `silicon_variant: TBD` per the project's no-inventing-values rule). Enables forward lookup of per-variant memory, optional features, and package-specific GPIO counts without reverse-scanning all SoC JSON files. Optional; when absent the loader falls back to reverse lookup via `alp_module_skus` in the SoC JSON.

on_module.description: Populated on-module chips + I2C bus topology.  The key set is CLOSED: every legal on-module role is enumerated below (union of all released families), so a new family inventing a synonym key (e.g. `pmic` for `pmic_main`) fails validation.  Adding a genuinely new on-module role means extending this enumeration deliberately, in the schema, with review.

silicon.description: Main silicon triple ref -- repeats the top-level `silicon:` for self-contained reads of the block.

pmic_main.description: Primary PMIC.  Canonical key on every family that populates one (imx93's former `pmic:` normalised to this).

pmic_secondary.description: Secondary PMIC (e.g. V2N's DA9292).  Absent on single-PMIC families.

supervisor_mcu.description: On-module supervisor / IO MCU (V2N family's GD32G553).  Absent on families without one.

ethernet_phy_count.description: Number of populated Ethernet PHY instances.  Only meaningful alongside `ethernet_phy:`; families with a single PHY (or none) omit it.

nor_flash.description: SoC controller / peripheral name that reaches the on-module NOR flash (routing annotation, not a chip slug -- e.g. `xspi`).

emmc.description: SoC controller / peripheral name that reaches the on-module eMMC (routing annotation -- e.g. `sd0`).

npu.description: Discrete on-module NPU chip (V2M's DEEPX DX-M1).  On-die NPUs (Ethos-U, DRP-AI) are silicon facts and do NOT appear here.

ospi_memories.description: Per-OSPI-instance external memory population (AEN family).  Keys are OSPI controller instances (`ospi0`, `ospi1`).

i2c_devices.description: Per-bus I2C device tables, allowed on every family.  Keys are bus identifiers (e.g. `brd_i2c`, `e1m_i2c0`) consumed by the SDK loader; the key set stays OPEN because bus names are a per-family fact (V2N's `brd_i2c` is a GD32-adjacent Renesas bus, AEN's is the Alif LPI2C0).

memory.description: Off-SoC module memory capacities, REQUIRED on every preset in one canonical shape.  `TBD` encodes 'pending the authoritative HW config' or 'per-BOM-variant' (per the no-inventing-values rule); on-die SRAM/MRAM is derived from the SoC variant, never declared here.

dram_mbit.description: External volatile memory in Mbit (LPDDR4X on V2N/V2M, HyperRAM on AEN).

flash_mbit.description: External non-volatile memory in Mbit (eMMC on V2N/V2M, OSPI NOR on AEN).

inference.description: Inference-accelerator selection.  One canonical shape: `preferred_backend` always; `ethos_u_variant` + `npu_population` are REQUIRED whenever the preferred backend is `ethos_u` (every Ethos-bearing SoM must enumerate its NPU instances -- the kconfig emitter reads the population list).  Non-Ethos backends (drpai, deepx_dxm1) have no per-instance population table.

preferred_backend.description: Canonical backend key (`ethos_u`, `drpai`, `deepx_dxm1`, ...).  Cross-checked against the device dispatcher by scripts/check_inference_backend_parity.py.

ethos_u_variant.description: Primary Ethos-U variant (`u55`, `u65`, `u85`) when multiple are populated.

npu_population.description: Per-instance NPU population.  REQUIRED for NPU-bearing (`ethos_u`) SoMs; each entry names the variant, the silicon's role label, and the host core/subsystem it pairs with.

capabilities.description: Per-SoM hardware-accelerator / feature capability flags.  DELIBERATELY OPEN keys (values pinned to booleans): the key set is matched against each library's metadata/library-profiles/<name>/hw-backends.yaml, which grows with the library catalogue -- enumerating it here would couple the preset schema to every library release.  ADDITIVE direction only: declare capabilities the SoM ADDS on top of the silicon (on-module chips, bridge features).  Removing a silicon capability the SKU leaves unpopulated goes in `silicon_capabilities.unpopulated` instead, never as a `false` here.

silicon_capabilities.description: SKU-level RESTRICTION of the referenced silicon's capability set.  A SKU can only remove (mark unpopulated / not-bonded-out) what its SoC JSON `capabilities:` block offers -- never add; additions belong in `capabilities:`.  Optional; when absent the SKU inherits the SoC's full capability set (the default for every current SKU).  Semantic cross-check (scripts/validate_metadata.py): every listed name must resolve to a truthy entry in the referenced SoC JSON's `capabilities:` block and must not also appear in this preset's `capabilities:` block.

unpopulated.description: Capability keys from the SoC JSON `capabilities:` block that this SKU does NOT populate (e.g. an accelerator fused off / not bonded out on this order code).  The loader's resolve_capabilities() forces each listed key to false/0 and scripts/gen_soc_caps.py drops the matching ALP_CAP_* flag for ALP_SOM_<SKU>-gated builds.

topology.description: Per-core default runtime + app mapping.  Keys are canonical `cores[].id` values from the resolved SoC spec (e.g. `a55_cluster`, `m33_sm`, `m55_hp`, `m55_he`, `a32_cluster`, `m33`).  Customer's board.yaml `cores:` block inherits + overrides.  Key set stays open here (it must mirror the SoC's cores[].id set, enforced by the pr-metadata-validate cross-check, not by this schema).

memory_map.description: OPTIONAL declarative memory-region table. Stock SoMs whose memory layout matches the silicon default omit this block; the loader derives effective regions from the SoC JSON variant's `sram_banks_kb` + `mram_mb` (resolved via `silicon_variant`). Declare entries here ONLY for non-stock partitioning (e.g. reserving SRAM for a hardware secure enclave outside the default layout). Per-app IPC carve-outs live in the customer's `board.yaml` `ipc:` block, not here.

mailbox.description: Vendor mailbox / IPC controller + channel reservations.  Required when any topology entry runs Zephyr or baremetal.  Per the project's standing convention, AEN / iMX93 controller name may be TBD until the user supplies the HW-config writeup.

controller.description: Vendor mailbox controller identifier (e.g. `renesas_mhu`, `nxp_mu`, `alif_mhuv2`).  TBD when the hand-written HW config still owes the value.

pad_routes.description: E1M pads / peripheral instances that route through a SoM-side mediator chip (e.g., CC3501E coprocessor on AEN, GD32G553 IO MCU on V2N). Pads NOT listed here are assumed to route directly to the main silicon's GPIO / peripheral. The SDK loader composes this list with the board's `e1m_routes:` block at codegen time: when an E1M pad appears in both blocks, the board supplies the role (e.g., `bmi323_int1`) and the SoM supplies the dispatch path (e.g., CC3501E GPIO 14).

helper_firmware.description: On-module helper MCUs whose firmware images the orchestrator registers into the system manifest.  These are NOT heterogeneous-compute peers (they don't appear in `topology:`); they're independently-flashed supervisor / coprocessor MCUs whose flash flow needs to be visible to `west alp-flash`.  Per the project's standing convention, AEN CC3501E entries stay TBD until the embedded `firmware/cc3501e/` tree (ADR 0015) cuts its first bench-built binary.

preliminary.description: True while the silicon / datasheet the preset encodes is itself preliminary.

partial_hw_config.description: True while any field in the preset is still TBD pending the user-supplied authoritative HW config.

oneOf.description: Chip-id slug matching a `metadata/chips/<chip>.yaml` manifest, or an SoC-controller routing annotation (`xspi`, `sd0`).
Part choice pending the user-supplied HW-config writeup / BOM decision.

chip.description: Storage-part MPN (e.g. `MX25UM25645GXDI00`), or the literal `TBD`.  MPN, not a chips/ slug: storage parts have no chip driver.

assembled.description: Population status: true (default), false (DNI), or "optional" (assembled per BOM variant).

interface.description: SoC controller instance the HyperRAM hangs off (e.g. `ospi0`).

role.description: Silicon's role label for the instance (e.g. `NPU-HE`, `NPU-HP`, `NPU-HG`).

paired_with.description: Host core / subsystem the instance is paired with (e.g. `M55-HE`, `HG-subsystem`).  `TBD` when the pairing awaits the HW-config writeup.

os.description: Default runtime for this core.  Customer's board.yaml `cores.<id>.os` overrides.

app.description: Stock app (`alp-image-edge` for Yocto A-class, `alp-stock-shim` for Zephyr M-class) or a path to a custom app directory.

machine.description: Yocto MACHINE name.  Required for `os: yocto` entries.

board.description: Zephyr board target name.  Required for `os: zephyr` entries.

zephyr_full_name.description: Human-readable board title for `os: zephyr` entries -- feeds `--emit zephyr-board`'s generated `board.yml` `full_name:`, the twister board `.yaml` `name:` (families that reuse it verbatim), and the board `.dts` header comment / `model =` property (parenthetical stripped). Free text so it stays a literal, reviewable fact rather than a string built by regex over other fields.

zephyr_twister_name.description: Twister board-metadata `name:` field for `os: zephyr` entries, when it differs from `zephyr_full_name` (some families drop the SoC parenthetical here). Defaults to `zephyr_full_name` when absent.

base.description: Physical base address.  TBD when the hand-written HW config still owes the value.

size_mib.description: Region size in MiB.  Provide either size_mib or size_kib.

size_kib.description: Region size in KiB.  Provide either size_mib or size_kib.

accessible_from.description: Core IDs that can access the region.  Must match keys in `topology:`.

cacheable.description: When true the region is cacheable from all `accessible_from` cores.  Default carve-out is non-cacheable.

dt_label.description: Optional Zephyr Devicetree label for this region.  Used by the storage emitter when board.yaml `storage:` entries reference this region as their `flash_device:`; the emitted overlay decorates `&<dt_label>` with a `partitions { ... }` child node.  Defaults to the region `name` when omitted.

name.description: Helper-MCU instance identifier (e.g. `gd32_bridge`, `cc3501e_otp`).  Unique within the SoM preset's helper_firmware list.

firmware_path.description: Repo-relative path to the prebuilt firmware image, or the literal string "TBD" pending an upstream release.

flash_method.description: Backend tool key consumed by `west alp-flash`'s helper-MCU dispatcher (e.g. `swd_probe`, `cc3501e_usb_bootloader`).  TBD pending the authoritative flow.

flash_args.description: Backend-specific args (interface, target, base address, ...) forwarded verbatim to the flash backend.  DELIBERATELY OPEN object: the shape follows `flash_method`'s backend contract, which this schema cannot enumerate; TBD when the method is TBD.

e1m.description: E1M-standard pad or peripheral instance identifier, e.g. `E1M_GPIO_IO15`, `E1M_SPI1`, `E1M_PWM0`.

dispatch.description: SoM-side mediator the pad routes through. `direct` is the default for pads not in the list; an explicit `direct` entry is allowed for clarity. `TBD` marks pads whose routing is pending the user's HW-config writeup (per the no-inventing-values rule).

dispatch_pin.description: Mediator-local pin or bus identifier. Forms: integer (CC3501E GPIO index, e.g. `14`); single pad name (`"PA11"` for a GD32 port pad); bus pin (`"SPI1.MISO"`); or a slash-joined pair for multi-pin peripherals (`"PA0/PB3"` for a quadrature encoder's X/Y pads).

doc.description: One-line free-form note (matches the e1m_routes entry shape on the board side).

bus_master.description: Which silicon drives the bus (informational).  Omitted when no on-module silicon masters the bus (e.g. AEN's slave-only LPI2C0 housekeeping bus, ADR 0017).

bus_pads.description: Per-silicon bus pad assignments (informational).  DELIBERATELY OPEN: keys name the silicon, values follow that vendor's pad-naming convention.

role.description: Role this chip plays on the bus, e.g. `pmic_main_page0`, `rtc`, `temp_sensor`.

oneOf.description: 7-bit I2C address as a hex string, e.g. "0x50".
Address pending the user-supplied HW-config writeup.
Address picked by the chip's firmware (e.g. GD32 supervisor MCU) -- not a hardware-fixed value.

assembled.description: Population status: true (default), false (DNI), or "optional" (assembled per BOM variant).

notes.description: Free-form note (rare in the public form -- design rationale belongs in the private metadata repo).
